query:
You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines repository.
The codebase is at version 1.7 (commit: d6f1403e).

## Issue to Resolve

recoverStackTrace causes nested exceptions
<!--
**Double-check**

* Is this *really* a bug?
  - If the behavior is documented, but you disagree with it, please do file an issue, but as a "Design consideration," not a "Bug report."
  - If you don't understand why something behaves the way it does, consider asking on [StackOverflow](https://stackoverflow.com/) or the [Kotlin Slack](https://surveys.jetbrains.com/s3/kotlin-slack-sign-up). The community is active and will likely clarify everything better than we could!
* Is the problem not in some third-party library, not in [Kotlin](kotl.in/issue), or your own codeâ€”is it in the `kotlinx.coroutines` library itself?
  - Example: you write for Android, and your code works properly on most devices, but for a couple of them, it fails. Then please direct this to Google and/or the manufacturer of your device.
* Maybe you're using some ancient version, and the problem doesn't happen with the latest releases of the compiler and the library?
-->

**Describe the bug**

When throwing an exception in a coroutine scope it sometimes becomes nested one level too deep in the enclosing exception.

For example, if I throw a: `TestException(IllegalStateException("test"))` (a TestException with a cause of IllegalStateException with a message), then the coroutine scope will throw an exception that is: `TestException(TestException(IllegalStateException("test")))`.

**Provide a Reproducer**

The following test will fail as a result of this bug:

```
@Test
fun testNestedException() = runTest {
    val result = runCatching {
        coroutineScope<Unit> {
            throw NestedException(IllegalStateException("ERROR"))
        }
    }
    val ex = result.exceptionOrNull() ?: error("Expected to fail")
    assertTrue(ex is NestedException)
    assertTrue(ex.cause is IllegalStateException)
    assertEquals("ERROR", (ex.cause as IllegalStateException).message)
}

class NestedException : RuntimeException {
    constructor(message: String) : super(message)
    constructor(cause: Throwable) : super(cause)
    constructor() : super()
}
```

I can get this test to not fail by changing the order of the constructors in the class to:

```
class NestedException : RuntimeException {
    constructor(cause: Throwable) : super(cause)
    constructor(message: String) : super(message)
    constructor() : super()
}
```
But I don't think this is actually a good solution as it shouldn't matter the order of the constructors in my exception, it should work the same way regardless.

I think the issue is at [this line](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/3fc1c20a87a689c09f062afc9e3ac347829d29f7/kotlinx-coroutines-core/jvm/src/internal/ExceptionsConstructor.kt#L30) where the wrong constructor is being selected (the string constructor instead of the cause constructor).

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Thank you for the report! The actual problem is in this line: https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/3fc1c20a87a689c09f062afc9e3ac347829d29f7/kotlinx-coroutines-core/jvm/src/internal/ExceptionsConstructor.kt#L44 Instead of ordering the constructors from best to worst, we exit when we find the first one that matches any viable signature.
These two constructors should be identical, something is going completely wrong here

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `.idea/codeStyles/Project.xml`
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/jvm/src/internal/ExceptionsConstructor.kt`
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/jvm/src/internal/StackTraceRecovery.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

## Issue to Resolve

recoverStackTrace causes nested exceptions
<!--
**Double-check**

* Is this *really* a bug?
  - If the behavior is documented, but you disagree with it, please do file an issue, but as a "Design consideration," not a "Bug report."
  - If you don't understand why something behaves the way it does, consider asking on [StackOverflow](https://stackoverflow.com/) or the [Kotlin Slack](https://surveys.jetbrains.com/s3/kotlin-slack-sign-up). The community is active and will likely clarify everything better than we could!

**Describe the bug**

```
@Test
fun testNestedException() = runTest {

        coroutineScope<Unit> {

    assertTrue(ex is NestedException)
    assertTrue(ex.cause is IllegalStateException)
    assertEquals("ERROR", (ex.cause as IllegalStateException).message)

    constructor(cause: Throwable) : super(cause)
    constructor() : super()
```

I think the issue is at [this line](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/3fc1c20a87a689c09f062afc9e3ac347829d29f7/kotlinx-coroutines-core/jvm/src/internal/ExceptionsConstructor.kt#L30) where the wrong constructor is being selected (the string constructor instead of the cause constructor).

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Thank you for the report! The actual problem is in this line: https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/3fc1c20a87a689c09f062afc9e3ac347829d29f7/kotlinx-coroutines-core/jvm/src/internal/ExceptionsConstructor.kt#L44 Instead of ordering the constructors from best to worst, we exit when we find the first one that matches any viable signature.
These two constructors should be identical, something is going completely wrong here

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `.idea/codeStyles/Project.xml`
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/jvm/src/internal/ExceptionsConstructor.kt`
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/jvm/src/internal/StackTraceRecovery.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

conversation_history.content:
1â†’/*
  2â†’ * Copyright 2016-2021 JetBrains s.r.o. Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 license.
  3â†’ */
  4â†’
  5â†’@file:Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
  6â†’
  7â†’package kotlinx.coroutines.internal
  8â†’
  9â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.*
 10â†’import _COROUTINE.ARTIFICIAL_FRAME_PACKAGE_NAME
 11â†’import _COROUTINE.ArtificialStackFrames
 12â†’import java.util.*
 13â†’import kotlin.coroutines.*
 14â†’import kotlin.coroutines.intrinsics.*
 15â†’
 16â†’/*
 17â†’ * `Class.forName(name).canonicalName` instead of plain `name` is required to properly handle
 18â†’ * Android's minifier that renames these classes and breaks our recovery heuristic without such lookup.
 19â†’ */
 20â†’private const val baseContinuationImplClass = "kotlin.coroutines.jvm.internal.BaseContinuationImpl"
 21â†’private const val stackTraceRecoveryClass = "kotlinx.coroutines.internal.StackTraceRecoveryKt"
 22â†’
 23â†’private val ARTIFICIAL_FRAME = ArtificialStackFrames().coroutineBoundary()
 24â†’
 25â†’private val baseContinuationImplClassName = runCatching {
 26â†’    Class.forName(baseContinuationImplClass).canonicalName
 27â†’}.getOrElse { baseContinuationImplClass }
 28â†’
 29â†’private val stackTraceRecoveryClassName = runCatching {
 30â†’    Class.forName(stackTraceRecoveryClass).canonicalName
 31â†’}.getOrElse { stackTraceRecoveryClass }
 32â†’
 33â†’internal actual fun <E : Throwable> recoverStackTrace(exception: E): E {
 34â†’    if (!RECOVER_STACK_TRACES) return exception
 35â†’    // No unwrapping on continuation-less path: exception is not reported multiple times via slow paths
 36â†’    val copy = tryCopyAndVerify(exception) ?: return exception
 37â†’    return copy.sanitizeStackTrace()
 38â†’}
 39â†’
 40â†’private fun <E : Throwable> E.sanitizeStackTrace(): E {
 41â†’    val stackTrace = stackTrace
 42â†’    val size = stackTrace.size
 43â†’    val lastIntrinsic = stackTrace.frameIndex(stackTraceRecoveryClassName)
 44â†’    val startIndex = lastIntrinsic + 1
 45â†’    val endIndex = stackTrace.frameIndex(baseContinuationImplClassName)
 46â†’    val adjustment = if (endIndex == -1) 0 else size - endIndex
 47â†’    val trace = Array(size - lastIntrinsic - adjustment) {
 48â†’        if (it == 0) {
 49â†’            ARTIFICIAL_FRAME
 50â†’        } else {
 51â†’            stackTrace[startIndex + it - 1]
 52â†’        }
 53â†’    }
 54â†’
 55â†’    setStackTrace(trace)
 56â†’    return this
 57â†’}
 58â†’
 59â†’@Suppress("NOTHING_TO_INLINE") // Inline for better R8 optimization
 60â†’internal actual inline fun <E : Throwable> recoverStackTrace(exception: E, continuation: Continuation<*>): E {
 61â†’    if (!RECOVER_STACK_TRACES || continuation !is CoroutineStackFrame) return exception
 62â†’    return recoverFromStackFrame(exception, continuation)
 63â†’}
 64â†’
 65â†’private fun <E : Throwable> recoverFromStackFrame(exception: E, continuation: CoroutineStackFrame): E {
 66â†’    /*
 67â†’    * Here we are checking whether exception has already recovered stacktrace.
 68â†’    * If so, we extract initial and merge recovered stacktrace and current one
 69â†’    */
 70â†’    val (cause, recoveredStacktrace) = exception.causeAndStacktrace()
 71â†’
 72â†’    // Try to create an exception of the same type and get stacktrace from continuation
 73â†’    val newException = tryCopyAndVerify(cause) ?: return exception
 74â†’    // Update stacktrace
 75â†’    val stacktrace = createStackTrace(continuation)
 76â†’    if (stacktrace.isEmpty()) return exception
 77â†’    // Merge if necessary
 78â†’    if (cause !== exception) {
 79â†’        mergeRecoveredTraces(recoveredStacktrace, stacktrace)
 80â†’    }
 81â†’    // Take recovered stacktrace, merge it with existing one if necessary and return
 82â†’    return createFinalException(cause, newException, stacktrace)
 83â†’}
 84â†’
 85â†’private fun <E : Throwable> tryCopyAndVerify(exception: E): E? {
 86â†’    val newException = tryCopyException(exception) ?: return null
 87â†’    // Verify that the new exception has the same message as the original one (bail out if not, see #1631)
 88â†’    // CopyableThrowable has control over its message and thus can modify it the way it wants
 89â†’    if (exception !is CopyableThrowable<*> && newException.message != exception.message) return null
 90â†’    return newException
 91â†’}
 92â†’
 93â†’/*
 94â†’ * Here we partially copy original exception stackTrace to make current one much prettier.
 95â†’ * E.g. for
 96â†’ * ```
 97â†’ * fun foo() = async { error(...) }
 98â†’ * suspend fun bar() = foo().await()
 99â†’ * ```
100â†’ * we would like to produce following exception:
101â†’ * IllegalStateException
102â†’ *   at foo
103â†’ *   at kotlin.coroutines.resumeWith
104â†’ *   at _COROUTINE._BOUNDARY._(CoroutineDebugging.kt)
105â†’ *   at bar
106â†’ *   ...real stackTrace...
107â†’ * caused by "IllegalStateException" (original one)
108â†’ */
109â†’private fun <E : Throwable> createFinalException(cause: E, result: E, resultStackTrace: ArrayDeque<StackTraceElement>): E {
110â†’    resultStackTrace.addFirst(ARTIFICIAL_FRAME)
111â†’    val causeTrace = cause.stackTrace
112â†’    val size = causeTrace.frameIndex(baseContinuationImplClassName)
113â†’    if (size == -1) {
114â†’        result.stackTrace = resultStackTrace.toTypedArray()
115â†’        return result
116â†’    }
117â†’
118â†’    val mergedStackTrace = arrayOfNulls<StackTraceElement>(resultStackTrace.size + size)
119â†’    for (i in 0 until size) {
120â†’        mergedStackTrace[i] = causeTrace[i]
121â†’    }
122â†’
123â†’    for ((index, element) in resultStackTrace.withIndex()) {
124â†’        mergedStackTrace[size + index] = element
125â†’    }
126â†’
127â†’    result.stackTrace = mergedStackTrace
128â†’    return result
129â†’}
130â†’
131â†’/**
132â†’ * Find initial cause of the exception without restored stacktrace.
133â†’ * Returns intermediate stacktrace as well in order to avoid excess cloning of array as an optimization.
134â†’ */
135â†’private fun <E : Throwable> E.causeAndStacktrace(): Pair<E, Array<StackTraceElement>> {
136â†’    val cause = cause
137â†’    return if (cause != null && cause.javaClass == javaClass) {
138â†’        val currentTrace = stackTrace
139â†’        if (currentTrace.any { it.isArtificial() })
140â†’            cause as E to currentTrace
141â†’        else this to emptyArray()
142â†’    } else {
143â†’        this to emptyArray()
144â†’    }
145â†’}
146â†’
147â†’private fun mergeRecoveredTraces(recoveredStacktrace: Array<StackTraceElement>, result: ArrayDeque<StackTraceElement>) {
148â†’    // Merge two stacktraces and trim common prefix
149â†’    val startIndex = recoveredStacktrace.indexOfFirst { it.isArtificial() } + 1
150â†’    val lastFrameIndex = recoveredStacktrace.size - 1
151â†’    for (i in lastFrameIndex downTo startIndex) {
152â†’        val element = recoveredStacktrace[i]
153â†’        if (element.elementWiseEquals(result.last)) {
154â†’            result.removeLast()
155â†’        }
156â†’        result.addFirst(recoveredStacktrace[i])
157â†’    }
158â†’}
159â†’
160â†’@Suppress("NOTHING_TO_INLINE")
161â†’internal actual suspend inline fun recoverAndThrow(exception: Throwable): Nothing {
162â†’    if (!RECOVER_STACK_TRACES) throw exception
163â†’    suspendCoroutineUninterceptedOrReturn<Nothing> {
164â†’        if (it !is CoroutineStackFrame) throw exception
165â†’        throw recoverFromStackFrame(exception, it)
166â†’    }
167â†’}
168â†’
169â†’@Suppress("NOTHING_TO_INLINE") // Inline for better R8 optimizations
170â†’internal actual inline fun <E : Throwable> unwrap(exception: E): E =
171â†’    if (!RECOVER_STACK_TRACES) exception else unwrapImpl(exception)
172â†’
173â†’internal fun <E : Throwable> unwrapImpl(exception: E): E {
174â†’    val cause = exception.cause
175â†’    // Fast-path to avoid array cloning
176â†’    if (cause == null || cause.javaClass != exception.javaClass) {
177â†’        return exception
178â†’    }
179â†’    // Slow path looks for artificial frames in a stack-trace
180â†’    if (exception.stackTrace.any { it.isArtificial() }) {
181â†’        @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
182â†’        return cause as E
183â†’    } else {
184â†’        return exception
185â†’    }
186â†’}
187â†’
188â†’private fun createStackTrace(continuation: CoroutineStackFrame): ArrayDeque<StackTraceElement> {
189â†’    val stack = ArrayDeque<StackTraceElement>()
190â†’    continuation.getStackTraceElement()?.let { stack.add(it) }
191â†’
192â†’    var last = continuation
193â†’    while (true) {
194â†’        last = (last as? CoroutineStackFrame)?.callerFrame ?: break
195â†’        last.getStackTraceElement()?.let { stack.add(it) }
196â†’    }
197â†’    return stack
198â†’}
199â†’
200â†’internal fun StackTraceElement.isArtificial() = className.startsWith(ARTIFICIAL_FRAME_PACKAGE_NAME)
201â†’private fun Array<StackTraceElement>.frameIndex(methodName: String) = indexOfFirst { methodName == it.className }
202â†’
203â†’private fun StackTraceElement.elementWiseEquals(e: StackTraceElement): Boolean {
204â†’    /*
205â†’     * In order to work on Java 9 where modules and classloaders of enclosing class
206â†’     * are part of the comparison
207â†’     */
208â†’    return lineNumber == e.lineNumber && methodName == e.methodName
209â†’            && fileName == e.fileName && className == e.className
210â†’}
211â†’
212â†’@Suppress("ACTUAL_WITHOUT_EXPECT")
213â†’internal actual typealias CoroutineStackFrame = kotlin.coroutines.jvm.internal.CoroutineStackFrame
214â†’
215â†’@Suppress("ACTUAL_WITHOUT_EXPECT")
216â†’internal actual typealias StackTraceElement = java.lang.StackTraceElement
217â†’
218â†’@Suppress("EXTENSION_SHADOWED_BY_MEMBER")
219â†’internal actual fun Throwable.initCause(cause: Throwable) {
220â†’    // Resolved to member, verified by test
221â†’    initCause(cause)
222â†’}
223â†’

content.text: Now let me look at the test case mentioned in the issue to better understand the problem:

128â†’    return result
129â†’}
130â†’
131â†’/**
132â†’ * Find initial cause of the exception without restored stacktrace.
133â†’ * Returns intermediate stacktrace as well in order to avoid excess cloning of array as an optimization.
134â†’ */
135â†’private fun <E : Throwable> E.causeAndStacktrace(): Pair<E, Array<StackTraceElement>> {
136â†’    val cause = cause
137â†’    return if (cause != null && cause.javaClass == javaClass) {
138â†’        val currentTrace = stackTrace
139â†’        if (currentTrace.any { it.isArtificial() })
140â†’            cause as E to currentTrace
141â†’        else this to emptyArray()
142â†’    } else {
143â†’        this to emptyArray()
144â†’    }
145â†’}
146â†’
147â†’private fun mergeRecoveredTraces(recoveredStacktrace: Array<StackTraceElement>, result: ArrayDeque<StackTraceElement>) {
148â†’    // Merge two stacktraces and trim common prefix
149â†’    val startIndex = recoveredStacktrace.indexOfFirst { it.isArtificial() } + 1
150â†’    val lastFrameIndex = recoveredStacktrace.size - 1
151â†’    for (i in lastFrameIndex downTo startIndex) {
152â†’        val element = recoveredStacktrace[i]
153â†’        if (element.elementWiseEquals(result.last)) {
154â†’            result.removeLast()
155â†’        }
156â†’        result.addFirst(recoveredStacktrace[i])
157â†’    }
158â†’}
159â†’
160â†’@Suppress("NOTHING_TO_INLINE")
161â†’internal actual suspend inline fun recoverAndThrow(exception: Throwable): Nothing {
162â†’    if (!RECOVER_STACK_TRACES) throw exception
163â†’    suspendCoroutineUninterceptedOrReturn<Nothing> {
164â†’        if (it !is CoroutineStackFrame) throw exception
165â†’        throw recoverFromStackFrame(exception, it)
166â†’    }
167â†’}
168â†’
169â†’@Suppress("NOTHING_TO_INLINE") // Inline for better R8 optimizations
170â†’internal actual inline fun <E : Throwable> unwrap(exception: E): E =
171â†’    if (!RECOVER_STACK_TRACES) exception else unwrapImpl(exception)
172â†’
173â†’internal fun <E : Throwable> unwrapImpl(exception: E): E {
174â†’    val cause = exception.cause
175â†’    // Fast-path to avoid array cloning
176â†’    if (cause == null || cause.javaClass != exception.javaClass) {
177â†’        return exception
178â†’    }
179â†’    // Slow path looks for artificial frames in a stack-trace
180â†’    if (exception.stackTrace.any { it.isArtificial() }) {
181â†’        @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
182â†’        return cause as E
183â†’    } else {
184â†’        return exception
185â†’    }
186â†’}
187â†’
188â†’private fun createStackTrace(continuation: CoroutineStackFrame): ArrayDeque<StackTraceElement> {
189â†’    val stack = ArrayDeque<StackTraceElement>()
190â†’    continuation.getStackTraceElement()?.let { stack.add(it) }
191â†’
192â†’    var last = continuation
193â†’    while (true) {
194â†’        last = (last as? CoroutineStackFrame)?.callerFrame ?: break
195â†’        last.getStackTraceElement()?.let { stack.add(it) }
196â†’    }
197â†’    return stack
198â†’}
199â†’
200â†’internal fun StackTraceElement.isArtificial() = className.startsWith(ARTIFICIAL_FRAME_PACKAGE_NAME)
201â†’private fun Array<StackTraceElement>.frameIndex(methodName: String) = indexOfFirst { methodName == it.className }
202â†’
203â†’private fun StackTraceElement.elementWiseEquals(e: StackTraceElement): Boolean {
204â†’    /*
205â†’     * In order to work on Java 9 where modules and classloaders of enclosing class
206â†’     * are part of the comparison
207â†’     */
208â†’    return lineNumber == e.lineNumber && methodName == e.methodName
209â†’            && fileName == e.fileName && className == e.className
210â†’}
211â†’
212â†’@Suppress("ACTUAL_WITHOUT_EXPECT")
213â†’internal actual typealias CoroutineStackFrame = kotlin.coroutines.jvm.internal.CoroutineStackFrame
214â†’
215â†’@Suppress("ACTUAL_WITHOUT_EXPECT")
216â†’internal actual typealias StackTraceElement = java.lang.StackTraceElement
217â†’
218â†’@Suppress("EXTENSION_SHADOWED_BY_MEMBER")
219â†’internal actual fun Throwable.initCause(cause: Throwable) {
220â†’    // Resolved to member, verified by test
221â†’    initCause(cause)
222â†’}
223â†’
